feature: Admin API secret exposed to browser bundle and localStorage
workflow_rationale: This is a security fix that replaces an insecure pattern (client-side secret + Bearer token) with a secure pattern (server-side session-based auth). The fix adds new secure code alongside existing code, then removes the old insecure code - matching the refactor workflow structure.

phases.name: Create Internal Admin Metrics API Route
phases.description: Create a new server-side API route at app/api/admin/monitoring/metrics/route.ts that uses Supabase session cookies for auth (no Bearer token needed). This route imports the metrics-collection logic from the existing metrics route.

subtasks.description: Create app/api/admin/monitoring/metrics/route.ts - server-side metrics endpoint using session auth

verification.expected: File exists with auth + metrics logic

subtasks.notes: Created app/api/admin/monitoring/metrics/route.ts with session-based authentication. File contains: getServerSession import, admin auth verification with role check, all metrics functions (system, cache, rateLimit, api, errors, users, database). Committed as 682376c.

phases.name: Refactor Monitoring Page to Use Secure Internal API
phases.description: Refactor app/admin/monitoring/page.tsx to remove localStorage usage, NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET reading, and Bearer token fetching. Replace with a simple fetch to the new internal /api/admin/monitoring/metrics route.

subtasks.description: Refactor app/admin/monitoring/page.tsx - remove secret/localStorage, use new internal API
subtasks.notes: Refactored app/admin/monitoring/page.tsx to use session-based authentication. Removed: localStorage.getItem('adminSecret'), NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET, Authorization header, and prompt() call. Now uses simple fetch to /api/admin/monitoring/metrics with SWR. Verification passed: grep returns 0 matches. Committed as e1e9e3d.

phases.name: Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET and Clean Up Env Config
phases.description: Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET from .env.local.example since it must never be exposed to the browser. Also remove the duplicate ADMIN_API_SECRET entry (keep only the server-only one).

subtasks.notes: Removed NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET=your-admin-secret-here from .env.local.example. Kept ADMIN_API_SECRET (server-only) as required. Verification passed: grep returns 0 matches. Committed as e0cfb6a.

phases.name: Update Existing Admin API Routes to Use Session Auth
phases.description: Update app/api/admin/metrics/route.ts and app/api/admin/cache-stats/route.ts to accept session-based auth (Supabase cookie session) as the primary auth mechanism, while keeping Bearer token support as a fallback for backward compatibility.

subtasks.description: Update app/api/admin/metrics/route.ts - add session auth, keep Bearer fallback
subtasks.notes: Updated app/api/admin/metrics/route.ts with session-based authentication. Added getServerSession import, updated verifyAdminAuth to check session with admin role, added Bearer token fallback for backward compatibility. Returns 401 for no auth, 403 for non-admin users. Verification passed: grep returns 16 matches of session/auth patterns. Committed as e480a54.

subtasks.description: Update app/api/admin/cache-stats/route.ts - add session auth, keep Bearer fallback
subtasks.notes: Added session auth via getServerSession() with Bearer fallback. All 3 handlers (GET, DELETE, POST) updated to use the same pattern as metrics/route.ts. Verification passed with 28 session-related occurrences.

phases.name: Integration Verification
phases.description: Verify end-to-end that the monitoring page loads metrics without any secret in the browser bundle or localStorage, and that the admin API routes work with both session and Bearer token auth.

subtasks.description: Verify monitoring page makes no secret requests - check bundle for exposed secrets

verification.instructions: Run 'npm run build' and check that: (1) the build succeeds, (2) grep the .next bundle for 'ADMIN_API_SECRET' and 'adminSecret' - both should return no results. Then run the app and verify the monitoring page loads correctly with metrics displayed.

subtasks.notes: Integration verification complete. Build succeeded (npm run build). Bundle security scan passed: grep for ADMIN_API_SECRET, adminSecret, and NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET in .next/static/ and .next/chunks/ returned 0 results - no secrets in client-side bundle. Monitoring page source verified: uses simple fetch to /api/admin/monitoring/metrics with session auth (no secret/Bearer token/localStorage). App run verification: requires Supabase/database access - code review confirms proper session-based auth flow.

acceptance_criteria: No localStorage usage in monitoring page for admin secret
No NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET references in monitoring page
Monitoring page loads and displays metrics correctly

verification_steps.name: Bundle Security Scan
verification_steps.expected_outcome: No secrets detected in browser bundle

verification_strategy.reasoning: Critical security fix - must verify no secret exposure in browser bundle and that monitoring page still functions correctly

checks: No adminSecret in localStorage calls in monitoring page
No NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_API_SECRET access in monitoring page
Build bundle contains no ADMIN_API_SECRET values
